name: FS 572: Fermentation Science & Technology
description: Graduate-level survey of food and beverage fermentation covering microbial metabolism enzyme kinetics yeast and bacterial cultures koji and mold fermentation lactic fermentation and distillation. Taught by Jacob Graves with faculty sponsor Dr. Patricia Liang. Lecture meets Tuesday and Thursday in Babcock Hall.

name: FS 572: Fermentation Lab
description: Hands-on laboratory section for FS 572. Students culture yeast and lactic bacteria build koji ferment vegetables and run small-batch distillation exercises while practicing lab safety and sanitation. Lab reports due weekly.

name: FS 572: Sensory Analysis Seminar
description: Discussion and sensory-analysis seminar for FS 572. Students evaluate fermented foods and beverages discuss readings from the Fermentation journal and develop a term paper on a fermented product of their choosing.

name: FS 572: Fermentation Science & Technology (Fall 2025)
description: Prior-year offering of FS 572. Survey of food and beverage fermentation from mash chemistry through distillation and sensory analysis. Archived for reference.

name: FS 572 Koji & Mold Fermentation Module 2026
description: Shared workspace for the FS 572 koji and mold fermentation module - protocols incubation data and process photos for student rice and soybean koji projects.

name: FS 572 Distillation & Spirits Module 2026
description: Workspace for the FS 572 distillation and spirits unit. Students run a pot-still exercise on a fermented corn and rice mash track each cut by ABV and sensory notes and document still setup and safety checks. Per-step pass/fail review under lab safety requirements.

name: FS 572 Distillation & Spirits Module 2025
description: Prior-year workspace for the FS 572 distillation and spirits unit. Retained for year-over-year comparison of student still runs cut quality and safety-check completion.
